title: Arrays Show Commutative Property
description: Recognize that arrays with rows and columns reversed have the same total.
condition: Given two arrays with dimensions a × b and b × a.
response: State that both arrays have the same total because a × b = b × a.
label: Principle / Model
example: 2 rows of 5 and 5 rows of 2 both equal $10$.

title: Compose Total from Equal Parts
description: Combine partial totals from equal groups to find an overall total.
condition: Given a number bond showing equal parts and their totals.
response: Add the part totals to find the whole.
label: Principle / Model

title: Describe Array by Rows and Columns
description: Identify the number of rows and columns in an array and state the structure.
condition: Given a rectangular array of objects.
response: State the number of rows of ___ and columns of ___.
label: Rule / Plan
example: An array shows 4 rows of 5 and 5 columns of 4.

title: Group Objects into Equal Sets
description: Physically or visually group objects into equal-sized sets.
condition: Given a collection of objects and a specified group size.
response: Circle or arrange the objects into groups of the given size.
label: Production / Skill

title: Identify Even Number by Ones Digit
description: Determine whether a number is even by checking its ones digit.
response: If the ones digit is 0, 2, 4, 6, or 8, state the number is even.
label: Rule / Plan
example: $18$ ends in $8$, so it is even.

title: Partition Rectangle into Equal Columns
description: Divide a rectangle into a specified number of equal columns and rows.
condition: Given a rectangle and a requirement such as '5 columns of 3.'
response: Draw partition lines to create the required equal columns and rows.
label: Production / Skill
example: Partition a rectangle into 5 columns with 3 in each column (total $15$).
